query:
INSERT IGNORE INTO growth_programs (id, name, slug, description, durationDays, category, status) VALUES (
  1,
  '7-Day Emotional Mastery',
  '7-day-emotional-mastery',
  'A transformative 7-day journey to understand, process, and master your emotional landscape. Each day builds on the last — moving from awareness to expression to integration.',
  7,
  'emotional-mastery',
  'active'
);

INSERT IGNORE INTO program_lessons (programId, day, title, concept, exercisePrompt, guidanceTemplate, `order`) VALUES
(1, 1, 'The Map of Your Inner World',
 'Emotions are not problems to solve — they are messengers carrying information about your needs, values, and boundaries. Today we map your emotional landscape without judgment.',
 'Close your eyes and scan your body right now. Where do you feel tension, heaviness, or aliveness? Name 3 emotions you''ve felt in the last 24 hours. For each one, write: What triggered it? Where did you feel it in your body? What was it trying to tell you?',
 'Acknowledge what they shared without fixing. Reflect back the emotion they named and ask what it needed.',
 1),
(1, 2, 'The Stories We Tell About Feelings',
 'We rarely experience emotions directly — we experience our interpretations of them. Today you''ll learn to separate the raw feeling from the story your mind wraps around it.',
 'Think of a recent moment when you felt a strong emotion. Write the story you told yourself about it (e.g., "They don''t care about me"). Now strip it back: what was the raw feeling underneath the story? What would change if you held the feeling without the story?',
 'Help them see the gap between the feeling and the interpretation. Ask: "What''s the feeling without the story?"',
 2),
(1, 3, 'Anger as a Compass',
 'Anger is one of the most misunderstood emotions. It is not aggression — it is a boundary signal. Today you''ll learn to read your anger as information rather than react to it.',
 'Think of something that made you angry recently — big or small. Write: What boundary was crossed? What value of yours was violated? If your anger could speak, what would it say it needs? What would honoring that need look like?',
 'Validate the anger first. Then guide them to the need underneath it.',
 3),
(1, 4, 'The Grief You Haven''t Named',
 'Grief isn''t only for death. We grieve lost relationships, versions of ourselves, dreams that didn''t happen. Unacknowledged grief becomes a weight we carry without knowing why.',
 'What are you grieving right now — even if it feels too small to call grief? Write about it without minimizing it. What did you lose? What did you hope for that didn''t happen? What would it mean to finally let yourself grieve this?',
 'Hold space for the grief. Don''t rush to silver linings. Ask: "What needs to be honored here?"',
 4),
(1, 5, 'Fear and the Future Self',
 'Fear is imagination pointed at threat. Today you''ll learn to distinguish between fear that protects you and fear that keeps you small — and meet the version of you on the other side.',
 'Name your biggest fear right now. Write it out fully. Now ask: Is this fear protecting me from real danger, or keeping me from growth? If I acted despite this fear, who would I become? Write a letter from your future self — the one who faced it.',
 'Explore the fear without dismissing it. Ask: "What would you do if you weren''t afraid?"',
 5),
(1, 6, 'The Language of Needs',
 'Most conflict and suffering comes from unmet needs we can''t articulate. Today you''ll build fluency in the language of your own needs — and learn to ask for what you actually need.',
 'Think of a relationship where you feel frustrated or disconnected. Write: What do I need from this person that I''m not getting? Have I ever clearly asked for it? What makes it hard to ask? Write out exactly how you would ask for this need to be met — directly and kindly.',
 'Help them name the need clearly. Ask: "What would it feel like to have that need met?"',
 6),
(1, 7, 'Integration: Your Emotional Signature',
 'You''ve spent 6 days mapping your emotional world. Today you integrate it all into your Emotional Signature — a living understanding of how you feel, what you need, and how you want to show up.',
 'Looking back at this week: What emotion showed up most? What did you learn about your needs? What old story are you ready to let go of? Write your Emotional Signature: 3 sentences about who you are emotionally, what you need to thrive, and how you commit to showing up for yourself.',
 'Celebrate the journey. Reflect back the growth you''ve witnessed. Ask: "What''s one thing you''ll carry forward?"',
 7);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway04.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user UbcpEQhTnXTazXN.root --database LQwmD5t86EFFZjkEDkXbgz --execute
